[Jun 25, 2026, 07:00-16:00 EST]

🔥 Top Stories
• Iran/Hormuz: shipping disruption resurfaces — reports of an IRGC strike on a Singapore-flagged cargo ship, UN evacuation pause, and renewed fee/toll rhetoric lifted oil and risk premia.
• US PCE/GDP reinforce higher-for-longer Fed risk — core PCE matched estimates, but headline PCE ran hot and Q1 GDP was revised up to 2.1%, keeping inflation worries alive.
• Apple sells off on broad price hikes — AAPL raised Mac/iPad/Vision Pro prices to offset memory costs, citing AI-driven chip demand; shares fell sharply.
• Micron surges on AI-memory boom — MU jumped ~18% on a strong forecast, lifting chip sentiment and briefly overtaking mega-cap peers in market value.

⛽ Oil & Energy
• WTI $71.92, Brent $75.26 — crude rebounded as Hormuz risk returned; analysts cut prior oil forecasts as transit normalizes, but fresh attacks keep volatility high.
• Hormuz fee plan + shipping recovery — Iran floated service fees while saying routes outside its system lose protection; traffic had been recovering before the latest attack.
• Gas storage builds — EIA natgas stocks rose 76 Bcf, a bit above estimates, keeping gas pressure contained.

📊 Markets & Macro
• Treasuries and gold caught mixed macro signals — US 10Y yield fell below 4.40% after PCE/GDP, while gold reclaimed $4,000/oz on softer yields and a weaker dollar.
• US data = resilient growth, sticky inflation — claims were light at 215k, spending rose 0.7%, but inflation stayed elevated enough to justify caution.
• Equities rotation continues — Nasdaq underperformed as megacap tech lagged; defensives, broader cyclicals and Europe outperformed.
• Dollar firmed — USD/JPY neared 162 as BofA turned more bullish on the dollar on solid US data and a hawkish Fed.

🌍 Geopolitical
• US-Iran talks continue, but trust remains thin — Rubio said Gulf states oppose Hormuz tolls and that Washington is watching actions, not rhetoric.
• Israel-Lebanon talks stall — Israeli officials say they will keep “security zones,” while Iran says a Lebanon withdrawal is a red line in any deal.
• Ukraine/NATO support reiterated — NATO said summit support for Ukraine is coming, while leaders also discussed weapons production and defense capacity.
• US trade diplomacy broadens — Washington reached a framework with Uzbekistan to cut tariffs and speed reciprocal trade talks.

🪙 Crypto & DeFi
• Bitcoin slides under $59k — ETF outflows and liquidations hit crypto sentiment; market fear is deepening and more BTC sits at unrealized loss.
• Base/crypto infrastructure wobble — Coinbase’s Base had an outage as Spark/Uniswap built a stablecoin FX layer and Kraken explored an Aave stake.

🏛️ Regulatory & Policy
• Fed officials push back on easing bets — Goolsbee and Williams warned inflation is still too high and the Fed remains well positioned, despite softer oil.
• SCOTUS keeps reshaping the policy backdrop — the Court let Trump-end DHS deportation protections and is set to rule on the Cook case next week.
• US and EU move on AI governance — Washington sought an AI partnership with the EU on rules and supply chains as OpenAI faces pressure to stagger model release.

🏢 Corporate
• Microsoft raises Xbox prices globally — console prices jump Aug. 1 as memory/storage costs more than doubled; management sees further increases ahead.
• Google ramps AI products and battles publishers — new Google Finance app/portfolios launch, while AI licensing terms stay hardline and a coding team is reorganized.
• Tesla expands Berlin hiring/output — plans call for more workers and a higher production ramp at the Berlin gigafactory.
• Bayer pops on legal win — the Supreme Court shielded Bayer from some Roundup warning suits, sending shares sharply higher.
